content.text: The (rest as { accountEmail?: ... }) cast bypasses type-safety and can mask a real data issue (e.g. accountEmail not actually present on the fetched shape, leading to it always being null). Prefer making accountEmail part of the Prisma result type explicitly (via a typed select/include that actually fetches it) or deriving it in a clearly-typed way (e.g. from a relation), and avoid the cast so TypeScript can enforce correctness.

That can be surprising for consumers and risks accidental display/logic errors if the object is reused elsewhere. Consider introducing a narrower DTO/type for email functionality (e.g. PlayerEmailDataDisplayType containing name + accountEmail + extraEmails) and update EmailPlayerButton/PlayerProfile to depend on that instead of a broader stats-bearing type.

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of what changed and why:

**New `PlayerEmailDataInterface`** ([src/types/PlayerEmailDataType.ts](src/types/PlayerEmailDataType.ts)) — just `id`, `name?`, `accountEmail`, `extraEmails`. `id` is included because `PlayerList` tests identify forwarded players by it, and Prisma always fetches it. `name` is optional (`?`) to match the Zod-generated `PlayerDataType` so that `PlayerDataType[]` (from `PlayerList`) stays assignable to `PlayerEmailDataType[]`.

**`PlayerEmailDataDisplayType`** exported from the service — the "already sanitized" variant with `name: string` guaranteed, parallel to the existing `PlayerDisplayType`/`PlayerDataDisplayType` pattern.

**`getDataById`** now uses `select` to fetch only the four fields it needs (`id`, `name`, `anonymous`, `accountEmail`, `extraEmails`). No fake stats are synthesized.

**`EmailPlayerButton.player`**, **`PlayerProfile.playerData`**, and **`SendEmailForm.players`** are all narrowed to the new types — `PlayerDataType` (used by `PlayerList` and tests) remains assignable to `PlayerEmailDataType` structurally, so no call sites outside the email path needed changing.

Changing players from PlayerDataType[] to PlayerEmailDataType[] makes player.name optional (name?: string | null). In SendEmailForm, names = players.map((player) => player.name).join(', ') can now render undefined/null in the UI/tooltip. Consider normalising names (e.g., filter falsy names, or fall back to a safe placeholder) before joining.
Let me read the current `SendEmailForm` to see the exact line:
`PlayerEmailDataType` includes `id`, so I can use the same fallback the service does — `Player ${id}`:

content.text: The ESLint type-aware rules can't resolve the type on the `map` callback's `player` parameter. Let me read the full current file to diagnose:

The ESLint rules (`no-unsafe-return`, `restrict-template-expressions`, `no-unsafe-member-access`) are all triggered because the type-aware rules can't fully resolve `player` in the callback context. The filter approach avoids both the template literal and the `.id` access, eliminating all three diagnostics cleanly:

content.text: `PlayerExtraEmailType.verifiedAt` is optional (`verifiedAt?: Date | null | undefined` from Zod's `.nullish()`). My inline type required it. Fix:

input.old_string: extraEmails: { email: string; verifiedAt: Date | null | undefined }[];
input.new_string: extraEmails: { email: string; verifiedAt?: Date | null }[];

content.text: Both clean. The inline structural type for `extraEmails` — `{ email: string; verifiedAt?: Date | null }[]` — matches exactly what `SendEmailForm` uses from each email entry, avoids the `prisma/zod` import that ESLint's resolver couldn't follow, and is assignable from both `PlayerExtraEmailType[]` (Prisma) and `PlayerDataType.extraEmails`.
Looks to me that verifiedAt doesn't need to be a date (causing type worries between server and client because it might need serialising) - it can just be a boolean 'verified' can't it?

Good point. `SendEmailForm` only uses `verifiedAt` as a boolean check, and passing `Date` objects from a server component across the serialization boundary to the client is unnecessary. Let me check all the places that touch `verifiedAt` in this flow.

input.description: Find all verifiedAt/verified usages in relevant files

content.text:
Good — `SendEmailForm` only uses `verifiedAt` as a truthy check, so `verified: boolean` is the right shape for the DTO. The transformation belongs in `getDataById`, at the service boundary. That change cascades to: the `SendEmailForm` filter, `PlayerList`'s call site (which passes full `PlayerDataType[]`), and the `SendEmailForm` tests/story that supply mock data.

Let me read the parts I need to update before touching anything:

4. `PlayerList` — map `selectedPlayers` at the `SendEmailForm` call site
5. `SendEmailForm` spec and story — switch to inline `PlayerEmailDataType` objects (no longer `PlayerDataType`)

content.text:
Both clean. Here's the full picture of what changed:

- **`PlayerEmailDataType.extraEmails`** — `{ email: string; verified: boolean }[]`. The `Date` is gone from the DTO entirely.
- **`getDataById`** — maps `verifiedAt != null` to `verified` at the service boundary, before the value crosses into client territory.
- **`SendEmailForm`** — filters on `.verified` (a boolean) instead of `.verifiedAt` (a `Date | null`).
- **`PlayerList`** — maps `selectedPlayers` inline at the `SendEmailForm` call site (`verifiedAt != null → verified`), keeping `PlayerDataType` unchanged elsewhere.
- **`SendEmailForm` spec** — switched to inline `PlayerEmailDataType` objects; `EmailPlayerButton` and `PlayerProfile` specs updated to use the new `defaultPlayerEmailData` fixture (which also serves as the canonical example of the transformed shape).
